tools.name: projects.containerProjectsAggregatedUsableSubnetworksList
tools.description: Lists subnetworks that are usable for creating clusters in a project.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsLocationsClustersCompleteIpRotation
tools.description: Completes master IP rotation.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsLocationsClustersCreate
tools.description: Creates a cluster, consisting of the specified number and type of Google Compute Engine instances. By default, the cluster is created in the project's [default network](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/networks-and-firewalls#networks). One firewall is added for the cluster. After cluster creation, the Kubelet creates routes for each node to allow the containers on that node to communicate with all other instances in the cluster. Finally, an entry is added to the project's global metadata indicating which CIDR range the cluster is using.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsLocationsClustersGetJwks
tools.description: Gets the public component of the cluster signing keys in JSON Web Key format. This API is not yet intended for general use, and is not available for all clusters.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsLocationsClustersList
tools.description: Lists all clusters owned by a project in either the specified zone or all zones.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsLocationsClustersNodePoolsCompleteUpgrade
tools.description: CompleteNodePoolUpgrade will signal an on-going node pool upgrade to complete.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsLocationsClustersNodePoolsCreate
tools.description: Creates a node pool for a cluster.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsLocationsClustersNodePoolsDelete
tools.description: Deletes a node pool from a cluster.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsLocationsClustersNodePoolsList
tools.description: Lists the node pools for a cluster.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsLocationsClustersNodePoolsRollback
tools.description: Rolls back a previously Aborted or Failed NodePool upgrade. This makes no changes if the last upgrade successfully completed.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsLocationsClustersNodePoolsSetAutoscaling
tools.description: Sets the autoscaling settings for the specified node pool.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsLocationsClustersNodePoolsSetManagement
tools.description: Sets the NodeManagement options for a node pool.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsLocationsClustersNodePoolsSetSize
tools.description: Sets the size for a specific node pool. The new size will be used for all replicas, including future replicas created by modifying NodePool.locations.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsLocationsClustersNodePoolsUpdate
tools.description: Updates the version and/or image type for the specified node pool.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsLocationsClustersSetAddons
tools.description: Sets the addons for a specific cluster.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsLocationsClustersSetLegacyAbac
tools.description: Enables or disables the ABAC authorization mechanism on a cluster.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsLocationsClustersSetLocations
tools.description: Sets the locations for a specific cluster. Deprecated. Use [projects.locations.clusters.update](https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.clusters/update) instead.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsLocationsClustersSetLogging
tools.description: Sets the logging service for a specific cluster.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsLocationsClustersSetMaintenancePolicy
tools.description: Sets the maintenance policy for a cluster.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsLocationsClustersSetMasterAuth
tools.description: Sets master auth materials. Currently supports changing the admin password or a specific cluster, either via password generation or explicitly setting the password.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsLocationsClustersSetMonitoring
tools.description: Sets the monitoring service for a specific cluster.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsLocationsClustersSetNetworkPolicy
tools.description: Enables or disables Network Policy for a cluster.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsLocationsClustersSetResourceLabels
tools.description: Sets labels on a cluster.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsLocationsClustersStartIpRotation
tools.description: Starts master IP rotation.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsLocationsClustersUpdateMaster
tools.description: Updates the master for a specific cluster.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsLocationsClustersWellKnownGetOpenidConfiguration
tools.description: Gets the OIDC discovery document for the cluster. See the [OpenID Connect Discovery 1.0 specification](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-discovery-1_0.html) for details. This API is not yet intended for general use, and is not available for all clusters.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsLocationsGetServerConfig
tools.description: Returns configuration info about the Google Kubernetes Engine service.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsLocationsOperationsCancel
tools.description: Cancels the specified operation.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsLocationsOperationsGet
tools.description: Gets the specified operation.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsLocationsOperationsList
tools.description: Lists all operations in a project in a specific zone or all zones.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsZonesClustersAddons
tools.description: Sets the addons for a specific cluster.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsZonesClustersCompleteIpRotation
tools.description: Completes master IP rotation.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsZonesClustersCreate
tools.description: Creates a cluster, consisting of the specified number and type of Google Compute Engine instances. By default, the cluster is created in the project's [default network](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/networks-and-firewalls#networks). One firewall is added for the cluster. After cluster creation, the Kubelet creates routes for each node to allow the containers on that node to communicate with all other instances in the cluster. Finally, an entry is added to the project's global metadata indicating which CIDR range the cluster is using.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsZonesClustersDelete
tools.description: Deletes the cluster, including the Kubernetes endpoint and all worker nodes. Firewalls and routes that were configured during cluster creation are also deleted. Other Google Compute Engine resources that might be in use by the cluster, such as load balancer resources, are not deleted if they weren't present when the cluster was initially created.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsZonesClustersGet
tools.description: Gets the details of a specific cluster.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsZonesClustersLegacyAbac
tools.description: Enables or disables the ABAC authorization mechanism on a cluster.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsZonesClustersList
tools.description: Lists all clusters owned by a project in either the specified zone or all zones.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsZonesClustersLocations
tools.description: Sets the locations for a specific cluster. Deprecated. Use [projects.locations.clusters.update](https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.clusters/update) instead.

tools.description: Sets the logging service for a specific cluster.

tools.name: projects.containerProjectsZonesClustersNodePoolsSetSize
tools.description: Sets the size for a specific node pool. The new size will be used for all replicas, including future replicas created by modifying NodePool.locations.

tools.description: Updates the version and/or image type for the specified node pool.

tools.description: Sets the maintenance policy for a cluster.
